# Vendored wikitext (MediaWiki markup) specification

`spec.wikitext` is the verbatim source of the [Help:Wikitext](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Wikitext)
page from the English Wikipedia, pinned to revision `1359831914` (dated 2026-06-17; see `VERSION`).
It is the reference description of wikitext — the markup language the MediaWiki software uses to format
pages — and documents every construct the reader targets: sections, lists, text formatting, links,
images, tables, templates, `nowiki`/`pre` escaping, comments, and variables.

The page text is authored by Wikipedia contributors and dual-licensed under the
[Creative Commons CC-BY-SA 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/) license (see
`LICENSE`) and the [GNU Free Documentation License](https://www.gnu.org/licenses/fdl-1.3.html).

It is vendored here as a public format specification — an allowed source of truth for this clean-room
implementation. The file is the page's own wikitext markup (`?action=raw`), so its syntax examples
survive intact; the plain-text rendering drops them. Only the syntax descriptions and example
**inputs** are used as the source of truth driving the reader; expected output is regenerated by the
pinned oracle binary, never copied from any rendered HTML.

Refresh or re-pin with `tools/fetch-mediawiki-spec.sh` (edit `SPEC_REVISION` to bump to a newer
revision id).
